1	/**
2	 * Lesson Duplication Orchestrator
3	 *
4	 * Drives the duplication pipeline for a single LessonDuplications record:
5	 *  1. K2: Select up to 20 exercises from source lesson (via selectExercisesScaled)
6	 *  2. For each exercise, concurrently (max 3):
7	 *     a. Route to strategy (script or AI) → generates new exercise content
8	 *     b. Run structural validator on the generated content
9	 *     c. If structural passes AND strategy != 'script', run semantic validator
10	 *     d. On any failure, append a failure entry to the LessonDuplications record
11	 *  3. After all exercises settle: set status=succeeded (0 failures) or needs_review (>0 failures)
12	 *
13	 * Failures are streamed live — each failure is written to the DB immediately
14	 * via payload.update(), not accumulated in memory.
15	 */
16	
17	import type { Payload } from 'payload'
18	import type { Exercise } from '@/payload-types'
19	import type { ContentBlock } from '@/server/payload/collections/Exercises/schemas'
20	import type { DuplicationSubject } from '@/server/payload/collections/LessonDuplications'
21	import { logger } from '@/infra/utils/logger'
22	import { selectExercisesScaled } from '@/server/services/lesson-duplication/selectors'
23	import { getSourceExercisesForLesson } from '@/server/services/lesson-duplication/source-exercises'
24	import {
25	  validateExerciseStructural,
26	  fillMissingFieldsWithPlaceholders,
27	  BLOCKING_FAILURE_CODES,
28	  type StructuralFailure,
29	} from '@/server/services/lesson-duplication/validators/structural'
30	import {
31	  validateExerciseSemantic,
32	  SEMANTIC_FAILURE_CODE,
33	} from '@/server/services/lesson-duplication/validators/semantic'
34	import { RouterStrategy } from '@/server/services/lesson-duplication/strategies/router'
35	
36	// Concurrency of 1 = process exercises sequentially. Each exercise hits the
37	// LLM twice (creative + deterministic). Gemini's per-minute quota is easily
38	// exceeded at higher concurrency, which triggered "rate limit exceeded"
39	// failures on every exercise in early multi-exercise runs. The variation
40	// service has rate-limit backoff as a safety net, but serializing is the
41	// primary control. Bump this back up only after we've sized the quota AND
42	// replaced appendEntry's read-modify-write with an atomic $push.
43	// `as const` pins the literal type so the compile-time guard below catches
44	// any bump (current value preserved as literal `1`).
45	export const CONCURRENCY_LIMIT = 1 as const
46	
47	export const GENERATION_FAILURE_CODE = 'GENERATION_FAILED' as const
48	
49	export type DuplicationStrategy = 'script' | 'ai'
50	
51	/** Result of running the strategy for one exercise. */
52	export interface StrategyResult {
53	  exerciseId: string
54	  strategy: DuplicationStrategy
55	  blocks: ContentBlock[]
56	}
57	
58	/**
59	 * Generate a new exercise via the appropriate strategy.
60	 *
61	 * K3: ScriptVariationStrategy for purely-algebraic exercises at light level.
62	 * K4: AiVariationStrategy for all other cases (throws until AI is wired up).
63	 *
64	 * @param exercise    Source exercise to vary
65	 * @param level       Duplication level (none/light/medium/deep)
66	 * @param _payload    Payload instance (available for future AI strategy use)
67	 * @returns           StrategyResult with generated blocks and strategy used
68	 */
69	export async function runStrategy(
70	  exercise: ExerciseDoc,
71	  level: 'none' | 'light' | 'medium' | 'deep',
72	  subject: DuplicationSubject,
73	  payload: Payload,
74	): Promise<StrategyResult> {
75	  const router = new RouterStrategy(payload)
76	  const result = await router.apply(exercise as unknown as Exercise, level, subject)
77	
78	  const content = result.exercise.content as unknown as { blocks: ContentBlock[] }
79	  return {
80	    exerciseId: exercise.id,
81	    strategy: result.needsAiFallback ? 'ai' : 'script',
82	    blocks: content.blocks ?? [],
83	  }
84	}
85	
86	/** Suggested action for a failure, based on failure code. */
87	function suggestAction(code: string): 'skip' | 'regenerate' | 'keep' {
88	  switch (code) {
89	    case 'MISSING_QUESTION':
90	    case 'MISSING_HINT':
91	    case 'MISSING_SOLUTION':
92	    case 'MISSING_FULL_SOLUTION':
93	    case 'MISSING_CORRECT_OPTION':
94	    case 'MISSING_WRONG_OPTIONS':
95	    case 'INVALID_SVG':
96	    case 'PNG_FORBIDDEN':
97	    case 'TOO_MANY_SECTIONS':
98	    case 'INVALID_GEOMETRY_SPEC':
99	    case 'INVALID_AXIS_SPEC':
100	    case 'INVALID_GUIDED_EXPLANATION':
101	      return 'regenerate'
102	    case 'SEMANTIC_MISMATCH':
103	      return 'regenerate'
104	    case 'GENERATION_FAILED':
105	      return 'skip'
106	    default:
107	      return 'skip'
108	  }
109	}
110	
111	// Compile-time guard: this whole module assumes CONCURRENCY_LIMIT === 1.
112	// appendEntry below uses a non-atomic read-then-update on the failures/warnings
113	// arrays — at concurrency >1 two parallel appends would race and lose entries.
114	// If you raise CONCURRENCY_LIMIT, replace appendEntry with a Mongo $push update
115	// before deleting this assert.
116	type _AssertConcurrencyOne = typeof CONCURRENCY_LIMIT extends 1 ? true : never
117	const _concurrencyAssert: _AssertConcurrencyOne = true
118	void _concurrencyAssert
119	
120	/**
121	 * Append a single failure/warning entry to the LessonDuplications record.
122	 *
123	 * The bucket name is chosen by the caller:
124	 *  - 'failures' = blocking; the exercise was dropped from the output lesson.
125	 *  - 'warnings' = non-blocking; the exercise was kept with TODO placeholders
126	 *    for the missing field. Admin polishes via the review screen.
127	 */
128	async function appendEntry(
129	  bucket: 'failures' | 'warnings',
130	  payload: Payload,
131	  duplicationId: string,
132	  exerciseRef: string,
133	  sectionIndex: number,
134	  code: string,
135	  message: string,
136	): Promise<void> {
137	  const action = suggestAction(code)
138	  try {
139	    const current = await payload.findByID({
140	      collection: 'lesson-duplications',
141	      id: duplicationId,
142	      depth: 0,
143	      overrideAccess: true,
144	    })
145	    await payload.update({
146	      collection: 'lesson-duplications',
147	      id: duplicationId,
148	      data: {
149	        [bucket]: [
150	          // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any
151	          ...(((current as any)[bucket] as any[]) ?? []),
152	          {
153	            exerciseRef,
154	            sectionIndex,
155	            code,
156	            message,
157	            suggestedAction: action,
158	            resolved: false,
159	          },
160	        ],
161	      } as never,
162	      overrideAccess: true,
163	    })
164	  } catch (err) {
165	    logger.error(
166	      { err, duplicationId, exerciseRef, code, bucket },
167	      `Failed to append ${bucket} entry to LessonDuplications`,
168	    )
169	    // Don't rethrow — streaming failure should not abort the run
170	  }
171	}
172	
173	/** Append a blocking failure (exercise dropped from output lesson). */
174	async function appendFailure(
175	  payload: Payload,
176	  duplicationId: string,
177	  exerciseRef: string,
178	  sectionIndex: number,
179	  code: string,
180	  message: string,
181	): Promise<void> {
182	  return appendEntry('failures', payload, duplicationId, exerciseRef, sectionIndex, code, message)
183	}
184	
185	/** Append a non-blocking warning (exercise kept with placeholder). */
186	async function appendWarning(
187	  payload: Payload,
188	  duplicationId: string,
189	  exerciseRef: string,
190	  sectionIndex: number,
191	  code: string,
192	  message: string,
193	): Promise<void> {
194	  return appendEntry('warnings', payload, duplicationId, exerciseRef, sectionIndex, code, message)
195	}
196	
197	/**
198	 * Append a successful output-exercise mapping to the LessonDuplications
199	 * record. Used to stream progress so a Vercel function timeout doesn't lose
200	 * work that's already in the DB. Read-modify-write on the record's
201	 * `outputExercises` array — safe under CONCURRENCY_LIMIT = 1.
202	 */
203	async function appendOutputExercise(
204	  payload: Payload,
205	  duplicationId: string,
206	  mapping: OutputExerciseMapping,
207	): Promise<void> {
208	  try {
209	    const current = await payload.findByID({
210	      collection: 'lesson-duplications',
211	      id: duplicationId,
212	      depth: 0,
213	      overrideAccess: true,
214	    })
215	    const existing =
216	      (current as unknown as { outputExercises?: OutputExerciseMapping[] }).outputExercises ?? []
217	    await payload.update({
218	      collection: 'lesson-duplications',
219	      id: duplicationId,
220	      data: { outputExercises: [...existing, mapping] } as never,
221	      overrideAccess: true,
222	    })
223	  } catch (err) {
224	    logger.error(
225	      { err, duplicationId, exerciseRef: mapping.sourceExerciseId },
226	      'Failed to append output exercise mapping',
227	    )
228	  }
229	}
230	
231	/** Shape of an exercise from the exercises collection. */
232	type ExerciseDoc = {
233	  id: string
234	  content?: { blocks?: ContentBlock[] }
235	}
236	
237	/** Mapping entry from source exercise to generated output exercise. */
238	interface OutputExerciseMapping {
239	  sourceExerciseId: string
240	  outputExerciseId: string
241	  strategy: DuplicationStrategy
242	}
243	
244	/**
245	 * Create the output lesson (draft) by deep-copying every source-lesson field.
246	 *
247	 * Earlier versions only copied title/slug/status/chapter/course/tenant/locale,
248	 * which dropped fields like accessType, visibleRenderers, blocks, contentStatus,
249	 * and any custom flags the source lesson had. This caused variation outputs to
250	 * be thinner than what the `none` endpoint produces. We now mirror the
251	 * deep-clone behaviour of the `none` path so both routes produce equivalent
252	 * lessons modulo the title suffix and a forced `draft` status.
253	 */
254	async function createOutputLesson(
255	  payload: Payload,
256	  sourceLessonId: string,
257	  level: string,
258	): Promise<string> {
259	  const source = await payload.findByID({
260	    collection: 'lessons',
261	    id: sourceLessonId,
262	    depth: 0,
263	    overrideAccess: true,
264	  })
265	  const sourceData = source as unknown as Record<string, unknown>
266	  // Drop Payload-managed + lineage fields; the rest carries over.
267	  //   slug:           regenerated by formatSlugAsync from the new title.
268	  //   blocks:         references source exercise IDs; rebuilt by the exercise
269	  //                   auto-add hook as new variation exercises are saved.
270	  //   translatedFrom: this is a fresh variation, not a translation copy.
271	  //   createdBy:      set to the duplicating admin via createdByField hook.
272	  const {
273	    id: _id,
274	    createdAt: _c,
275	    updatedAt: _u,
276	    slug: _ignoreSlug,
277	    blocks: _ignoreBlocks,
278	    translatedFrom: _ignoreTranslatedFrom,
279	    createdBy: _ignoreCreatedBy,
280	    ...rest
281	  } = sourceData as Record<string, unknown> & {
282	    id?: unknown
283	    createdAt?: unknown
284	    updatedAt?: unknown
285	    slug?: unknown
286	    blocks?: unknown
287	    translatedFrom?: unknown
288	    createdBy?: unknown
289	  }
290	  void _id
291	  void _c
292	  void _u
293	  void _ignoreSlug
294	  void _ignoreBlocks
295	  void _ignoreTranslatedFrom
296	  void _ignoreCreatedBy
297	  const base = (rest.title as string) ?? 'Lesson'
298	  const newLesson = await payload.create({
299	    collection: 'lessons',
300	    data: {
301	      ...rest,
302	      title: `${base} - Variation (${level})`,
303	      status: 'draft', // never publish a duplicate by default
304	    } as never,
305	    overrideAccess: true,
306	  })
307	  return newLesson.id
308	}
309	
310	/**
311	 * Create a draft exercise in the output lesson with the generated blocks.
312	 * Returns the mapping entry for tracking.
313	 */
314	async function createOutputExercise(
315	  payload: Payload,
316	  result: StrategyResult,
317	  outputLessonId: string,
318	): Promise<OutputExerciseMapping> {
319	  const ex = await payload.create({
320	    collection: 'exercises',
321	    data: {
322	      title: `Variation of ${result.exerciseId}`,
323	      lesson: outputLessonId,
324	      content: { blocks: result.blocks },
325	      status: 'draft',
326	    } as never,
327	    overrideAccess: true,
328	  })
329	  return {
330	    sourceExerciseId: result.exerciseId,
331	    outputExerciseId: ex.id,
332	    strategy: result.strategy,
333	  }
334	}
335	
336	/**
337	 * Process a single exercise through the duplication pipeline.
338	 *
339	 * Steps:
340	 *  1. Run strategy → get new exercise content
341	 *  2. Structural validation → collect failures
342	 *  3. If structural passes AND strategy != 'script' → semantic validation
343	 *  4. On any failure: stream failure to DB, return null
344	 *  5. On all pass: return StrategyResult for the orchestrator to use
345	 */
346	async function processExercise(
347	  exercise: ExerciseDoc,
348	  duplicationId: string,
349	  level: 'none' | 'light' | 'medium' | 'deep',
350	  subject: DuplicationSubject,
351	  payload: Payload,
352	): Promise<StrategyResult | null> {
353	  const exerciseRef = exercise.id
354	
355	  // Step 1: Run strategy
356	  let strategyResult: StrategyResult
357	  try {
358	    strategyResult = await runStrategy(exercise, level, subject, payload)
359	  } catch (err) {
360	    const message = err instanceof Error ? err.message : 'Unknown strategy error'
361	    logger.error({ exerciseRef, level, err }, 'Strategy generation failed')
362	    // Fire-and-forget: don't await — a slow DB write here would otherwise
363	    // block the entire concurrency loop and cause test timeouts under load.
364	    appendFailure(payload, duplicationId, exerciseRef, 0, GENERATION_FAILURE_CODE, message)
365	    return null
366	  }
367	
368	  // Step 2: Structural validation. Split failures into blocking (drop the
369	  // exercise — renderer would crash) and warnings (admin fills from review
370	  // screen). Warning-only exercises still ship, with TODO placeholders filled
371	  // in for missing hint/solution/fullSolution so the lesson stays renderable.
372	  const structuralFailures: StructuralFailure[] = validateExerciseStructural(strategyResult.blocks)
373	  const blockingFailures = structuralFailures.filter((f) => BLOCKING_FAILURE_CODES.has(f.code))
374	  const warningFailures = structuralFailures.filter((f) => !BLOCKING_FAILURE_CODES.has(f.code))
375	
376	  // Record blocking failures and non-blocking warnings into separate buckets
377	  // so the review UI can show them under different headings.
378	  // Fire-and-forget: don't await — slow DB writes here would block the
379	  // concurrency loop and cause timeouts when processing many exercises.
380	  for (const failure of blockingFailures) {
381	    appendFailure(
382	      payload,
383	      duplicationId,
384	      exerciseRef,
385	      failure.blockIndex ?? 0,
386	      failure.code,
387	      failure.message,
388	    )
389	  }
390	  for (const warning of warningFailures) {
391	    appendWarning(
392	      payload,
393	      duplicationId,
394	      exerciseRef,
395	      warning.blockIndex ?? 0,
396	      warning.code,
397	      warning.message,
398	    )
399	  }
400	
401	  if (blockingFailures.length > 0) {
402	    return null
403	  }
404	
405	  // Warning-only path: fill placeholders so the exercise renders. Warnings are
406	  // already recorded so the admin can find the TODOs in the review screen.
407	  if (warningFailures.length > 0) {
408	    strategyResult = {
409	      ...strategyResult,
410	      blocks: fillMissingFieldsWithPlaceholders(strategyResult.blocks),
411	    }
412	  }
413	
414	  // Step 3: Semantic validation. Skipped for:
415	  //  - level=none (deep clone, no AI variation to judge)
416	  //  - script strategy (deterministic, no hallucination risk)
417	  //  - warning-only path (we just inserted "_TODO: hint not provided by AI_"
418	  //    placeholders; a semantic reviewer would flag those as nonsensical and
419	  //    demote an exercise that should ship with a polish-later flag into a
420	  //    hard failure)
421	  if (strategyResult.strategy !== 'script' && level !== 'none' && warningFailures.length === 0) {
422	    const semanticResult = await validateExerciseSemantic(
423	      strategyResult.blocks,
424	      level,
425	      strategyResult.strategy,
426	      payload,
427	    )
428	    if (!semanticResult.ok) {
429	      // Fire-and-forget: slow DB write must not block the concurrency loop.
430	      appendFailure(
431	        payload,
432	        duplicationId,
433	        exerciseRef,
434	        0,
435	        SEMANTIC_FAILURE_CODE,
436	        `Semantic mismatch: ${semanticResult.reasons.join('; ')}`,
437	      )
438	      return null
439	    }
440	  }
441	
442	  return strategyResult
443	}
444	
445	/**
446	 * Reserve at least this much wall time (ms) at the end of a tick so the
447	 * orchestrator can write its final state to the DB before the platform
448	 * (Vercel) yanks the function. 30s covers a slow Mongo round-trip plus
449	 * Vercel's teardown grace.
450	 */
451	const FINALIZE_BUDGET_MS = 30_000
452	
453	/**
454	 * Default assumed worst-case time for one exercise (pass-1 + pass-2 +
455	 * validators on gemini-3.1-pro-preview). Used to decide whether the next
456	 * exercise can fit before the deadline. Conservative — if the run is
457	 * faster, we just process more per tick.
458	 */
459	const ASSUMED_EXERCISE_DURATION_MS = 300_000
460	
461	export type RunOutcome = 'succeeded' | 'needs_review' | 'failed' | 'in_progress'
462	
463	export interface RunOptions {
464	  /**
465	   * Absolute wall-clock deadline (Date.now() ms). The orchestrator returns
466	   * `in_progress` as soon as the remaining time is too short to fit another
467	   * exercise — leaving the record in `running` so the next cron tick picks
468	   * up where this one left off. Default: no deadline (process all).
469	   */
470	  deadlineMs?: number
471	}
472	
473	/**
474	 * Orchestrate the duplication pipeline for a single LessonDuplications record.
475	 * Resumable: skips exercises already processed in previous ticks and streams
476	 * each new output mapping to the DB as it completes. A Vercel function
477	 * timeout mid-run leaves a clean partial state — the next cron tick continues
478	 * from there.
479	 *
480	 * @param duplicationId  ID of the LessonDuplications record to process
481	 * @param payload        Payload instance
482	 * @param options.deadlineMs  Absolute timestamp by which to stop processing
483	 *
484	 * Pre-condition: record has status `pending` (fresh) or `running` (resuming).
485	 * Post-condition (returned outcome):
486	 *  - 'succeeded'   — all picked exercises produced an output, 0 failures
487	 *  - 'needs_review'— all picked exercises terminal, ≥1 failure
488	 *  - 'in_progress' — deadline hit, more work to do, status left as 'running'
489	 *  - 'failed'      — fatal error before any exercises processed
490	 */
491	export async function runDuplicationOrchestrator(
492	  duplicationId: string,
493	  payload: Payload,
494	  options: RunOptions = {},
495	): Promise<RunOutcome> {
496	  // Load the duplication record
497	  const duplication = await payload.findByID({
498	    collection: 'lesson-duplications',
499	    id: duplicationId,
500	    depth: 1, // need sourceLesson relationship
501	    overrideAccess: true,
502	  })
503	
504	  if (!duplication) {
505	    logger.error({ duplicationId }, 'LessonDuplications record not found')
506	    return 'failed'
507	  }
508	
509	  // Already terminal — nothing to do.
510	  if (
511	    duplication.status === 'succeeded' ||
512	    duplication.status === 'needs_review' ||
513	    duplication.status === 'failed'
514	  ) {
515	    logger.warn(
516	      { duplicationId, status: duplication.status },
517	      'LessonDuplications record already terminal, skipping',
518	    )
519	    return duplication.status as RunOutcome
520	  }
521	
522	  // Accept both 'pending' (first tick) and 'running' (resuming) — anything
523	  // else is unexpected.
524	  if (duplication.status !== 'pending' && duplication.status !== 'running') {
525	    logger.warn(
526	      { duplicationId, status: duplication.status },
527	      'LessonDuplications record in unexpected status, skipping',
528	    )
529	    return 'failed'
530	  }
531	
532	  try {
533	    const sourceLessonId =
534	      typeof duplication.sourceLesson === 'string'
535	        ? duplication.sourceLesson
536	        : (duplication.sourceLesson as { id?: string })?.id
537	
538	    if (!sourceLessonId) {
539	      throw new Error('sourceLesson relationship is missing or invalid')
540	    }
541	
542	    // First tick: create output lesson, flip to running. Resuming tick:
543	    // reuse the existing output lesson recorded on the record.
544	    let outputLessonId: string
545	    const existingOutputLessonRef = duplication.outputLesson as
546	      | string
547	      | { id?: string }
548	      | null
549	      | undefined
550	    const existingOutputLessonId =
551	      typeof existingOutputLessonRef === 'string'
552	        ? existingOutputLessonRef
553	        : existingOutputLessonRef?.id
554	
555	    if (duplication.status === 'pending') {
556	      outputLessonId = await createOutputLesson(
557	        payload,
558	        sourceLessonId,
559	        duplication.level ?? 'light',
560	      )
561	      await payload.update({
562	        collection: 'lesson-duplications',
563	        id: duplicationId,
564	        data: { status: 'running', outputLesson: outputLessonId } as never,
565	        overrideAccess: true,
566	      })
567	    } else {
568	      // resuming
569	      if (!existingOutputLessonId) {
570	        // Shouldn't happen — running record without an outputLesson means a
571	        // previous tick crashed before the initial write. Treat as fresh.
572	        outputLessonId = await createOutputLesson(
573	          payload,
574	          sourceLessonId,
575	          duplication.level ?? 'light',
576	        )
577	        await payload.update({
578	          collection: 'lesson-duplications',
579	          id: duplicationId,
580	          data: { outputLesson: outputLessonId } as never,
581	          overrideAccess: true,
582	        })
583	      } else {
584	        outputLessonId = existingOutputLessonId
585	      }
586	    }
587	
588	    const allExercises = await getSourceExercisesForLesson(payload, sourceLessonId)
589	
590	    // No artificial cap — resumability handles long runs across multiple
591	    // cron ticks. selectExercisesScaled still bounds at 20 per the original
592	    // product spec (admin scaling-random across the whole lesson).
593	    const PER_RUN_EXERCISE_CAP = 20
594	    const selectedExercises = selectExercisesScaled(
595	      allExercises as ExerciseDoc[],
596	      PER_RUN_EXERCISE_CAP,
597	    )
598	
599	    // Filter out exercises already processed on prior ticks. Sources of truth:
600	    //  1. The record's `outputExercises` — explicitly recorded successes.
601	    //  2. The record's `failures` — terminal per-exercise failures.
602	    //  3. Output exercises already present in the output lesson itself, even
603	    //     if their mapping never made it back into `outputExercises`. This
604	    //     closes the crash-window between createOutputExercise (writes the
605	    //     exercise to Mongo) and appendOutputExercise (records the mapping):
606	    //     if the function dies between those two calls, the exercise is
607	    //     durably saved but the record doesn't know about it; without this
608	    //     check the next tick would re-process the source and create a
609	    //     duplicate. We identify the source by the deterministic title
610	    //     `Variation of <sourceExerciseId>` set in createOutputExercise.
611	    const existingOutputExercises = await payload.find({
612	      collection: 'exercises',
613	      where: { lesson: { equals: outputLessonId } },
614	      limit: 0,
615	      depth: 0,
616	      overrideAccess: true,
617	    })
618	    const titleSourceIds = new Set<string>()
619	    const orphanMappings: OutputExerciseMapping[] = []
620	    const knownMappingSources = new Set(
621	      ((duplication.outputExercises as unknown as OutputExerciseMapping[]) ?? []).map(
622	        (m) => m.sourceExerciseId,
623	      ),
624	    )
625	    for (const ex of existingOutputExercises.docs as Array<{
626	      id: string
627	      title?: string
628	    }>) {
629	      const match = typeof ex.title === 'string' ? /^Variation of (\S+)/.exec(ex.title) : null
630	      if (match) {
631	        titleSourceIds.add(match[1])
632	        // Recover the mapping for any orphan exercise (created but never
633	        // recorded). Next tick start, we'll reconcile by appending these
634	        // mappings so the record matches reality going forward.
635	        if (!knownMappingSources.has(match[1])) {
636	          orphanMappings.push({
